basics.name: Sandip Dutta
basics.label: Software Development Engineer
basics.summary: Software Development Engineer at Amazon working on ML infrastructure, AI agents, and backend systems. Previously Research SWE at IIT Kharagpur's VIP Lab, with publications in medical image segmentation and vision transformers.

work.name: Amazon
work.summary: Bengaluru, India. Backend, ML infrastructure, and AI agent systems serving global marketplaces.
highlights: Own the delivery-time-prediction ML pipeline serving 3+ marketplaces; designed the training architecture on 20M+ records and drove integration across 3 partner teams, lifting prediction accuracy 15%.
Designed and shipped a React-based seller-facing prompt end-to-end across partner team systems, reducing missing items in multi-item orders and saving $650K+ annually.
Architected an AI agent that automates SQL editing, testing, and monitoring on a data warehouse platform, cutting manual data engineering work by 25%.
Led design and delivery of a company-wide AI-powered self-serve tool for auditing merchant flags across marketplaces, cutting support tickets by 20%.
Lowered infrastructure costs by 25% via EC2 right-sizing and auto-scaling; mentored a 2-month SDE intern from onboarding through launch.

work.name: IIT Kharagpur — VIP Lab
work.summary: Kharagpur, India. Medical imaging and deep-learning research with clinical deployments.
highlights: Built a CLI tool that auto-segments radiotherapy structures in CT scans via parallel processing, cutting manual annotation time by 6x.
Consulted on rollout across 3 hospitals including Tata Medical Center, Kolkata, standardizing the imaging pipeline for multi-hospital compatibility.
Co-authored FFLUNet, a brain-tumor segmentation model using 95% fewer parameters than existing approaches, published in Computers in Biology and Medicine.
Co-authored DualViT, a vision-transformer image classification model, published at ICPR 2024.

work.name: AwesomeSuite Inc.
highlights: Cut API latency 40% by resolving N+1 queries, optimizing SQL, and adding caching.
Built a document conversion service on AWS Lambda and an end-to-end digital signing workflow.

work.name: Amazon
highlights: Automated tax-document metadata extraction, eliminating 75% of manual work and accelerating reporting 80%.
Cut data-pipeline runtime by 40% via optimized migration scripts.

awards.title: Smart India Hackathon 2022 — Finalist
awards.awarder: Government of India, Ministry of Education
awards.summary: Shortlisted to the national finals for CDIApp, a crop-disease detection system combining CNN-based image classification with a Gemini-powered remedy recommender.

publications.name: FFLUNet: Feature Fused Lightweight UNet for Brain Tumor Segmentation
publications.publisher: Computers in Biology and Medicine, vol. 194, art. 110460
publications.summary: Kundu S, Dutta S, Mukhopadhyay J, Chakravorty N. Lightweight UNet variant achieving competitive brain-tumor segmentation accuracy with ~95% fewer parameters than prior state-of-the-art.

publications.name: Deep learning for Radiotherapy Autosegmentation Workflow (DRAW)
publications.publisher: The Royal College of Radiologists Open, vol. 3, art. 100210
publications.summary: Chakraborty S, Mallick I, Dutta S, Mukhopadhyay J, Kundu S, Maity S, et al. End-to-end deep-learning autosegmentation workflow for radiotherapy planning, built on open-source components and validated across multiple hospitals.

publications.name: DualViT: A Hierarchical Vision Transformer for Broad and Fine Class Embeddings
publications.publisher: International Conference on Pattern Recognition (ICPR) 2024, pp. 35–49
publications.summary: Chatterjee A, Dutta S, Mukhopadhyay J, Das PP. Hierarchical dual-branch ViT that jointly learns broad and fine-grained class embeddings for image classification.

publications.name: An Efficient BERT Based Approach to Detect Aggression and Misogyny
publications.publisher: 18th International Conference on Natural Language Processing (ICON) 2021
publications.summary: Dutta S, Majumder U, Naskar SK. BERT + Tf-Idf + XGBoost pipeline for aggression and misogyny detection on social-media text.

publications.name: Sdutta at CommA@ICON: A CNN-LSTM Model for Hate Detection
publications.publisher: ICON 2021 — Shared Task on Multilingual Gender Biased and Communal Language Identification
publications.summary: Dutta S, Majumder U, Naskar SK. CNN-LSTM hybrid for multilingual aggression, misogyny, and communal-language detection.

skills.name: Infrastructure & Tooling
keywords: AWS (ECS, EC2, Lambda, S3, SNS, SQS)

projects.name: CDIApp — Crop Disease Detection
projects.description: Image-classification model (95% accuracy) that identifies crop diseases from leaf photos, paired with a Gemini-powered remedy recommender. Shortlisted for Smart India Hackathon 2022 finals.
highlights: CNN-based classifier trained on public plant-disease datasets
LLM-backed remedy suggestions via Google Gemini API
